briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for April 16, 2026. Based on the 213 climate-related news items collected today, we will delve into the severity of current climate change, the diverse domestic and international efforts to address it, and the role of future technologies.

sections.title: 🔴 Climate Crisis Warnings and Immediate Challenges
sections.content: Threats from climate change are becoming increasingly visible recently. Domestically, climate change has led to an earlier and more diverse emergence of pests, with outbreaks of lovebugs and cyst nematodes causing direct damage to agriculture and ecosystems [1, 25, 33]. Internationally, warnings have emerged that the potential collapse of the Atlantic Meridional Overturning Circulation (AMOC) is much greater than previously anticipated, which could lead to catastrophic climate changes such as extreme cold and drought [2, 212]. Furthermore, climate change is threatening nighttime temperatures and infrastructure in Minnesota, and global health threats are materializing, including the addition of emperor penguins to the endangered species list [51, 193, 202, 208, 209]. Amidst these circumstances, news of the Trump administration's reversal of climate change rulings and Delta Air Lines abandoning its climate goals suggests a retreat at policy and corporate levels, adding to concerns [195, 204].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Promoting Domestic Climate Change Awareness and Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Domestic activities to raise awareness of the climate crisis and encourage carbon neutrality practices are active. Various local governments and organizations, including Yongin City, Gwangju City, Yeongsan River Environmental Office, Pocheon City, Namhae County, Gangdong-gu, and Osan City, are operating 'Climate Change Week' around Earth Day (April 22), conducting diverse citizen-participatory carbon neutrality programs [3, 4, 5, 7, 11, 15, 17, 19, 20, 22, 23, 26, 27, 30, 35, 36, 41, 42, 44, 46, 49, 52, 54, 56, 59, 95, 97, 126, 132]. In particular, Yongin Special City and Gapyeong County emphasized the importance of energy saving through lights-out campaigns, and Kookmin University's HUSS Climate Change Response Project Group organized plogging and environmental campaigns for 'Clean Week,' encouraging practical participation [27, 31, 32, 42, 44, 48, 55]. Furthermore, the UNFCCC Climate Week opened in Yeosu, taking center stage in global climate diplomacy and initiating discussions on green transition [8, 39, 45, 47, 50, 60].

sections.title: 🌱 Expanding Investment in Climate Technology and Carbon Neutrality Policy Trends
sections.content: Governments and corporations are actively investing in technology development and the realization of carbon neutrality to address climate change. The Ministry of Science and ICT plans to invest 3.4 trillion won, a 14.1% increase from last year, in R&D for climate change response this year, expanding the development of greenhouse gas reduction technologies, solar cells, and SMRs (Small Modular Reactors) [6, 12, 13, 14, 18, 28, 34, 38, 43, 57, 133, 207]. Hyundai Department Store Group held a ceremony for the creation of its second 'Carbon Neutral Forest' in Yongin, planning to plant 40,000 trees by 2029, which is evaluated as part of its expanded ESG management [65, 66, 67, 72, 74, 75, 77, 99, 100, 101, 108, 109, 112, 113, 114, 116, 117, 119, 121, 123, 125, 131]. Furthermore, KTR is collaborating with nine verification bodies to help industrial complexes achieve carbon neutrality, and a research team at Sungkyunkwan University is presenting a new solution for carbon neutrality with 'gas batteries' utilizing greenhouse gases, demonstrating active technological innovation [80, 96, 104, 120, 127, 130].

sections.title: 🌍 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Challenges
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating both domestically and internationally, but it simultaneously faces several challenges. Kolon Global has completed a 17.6MW wind power complex in Taebaek, accelerating the expansion of its renewable energy business [137, 147, 153, 162, 176, 184, 187, 188]. Samsung Asset Management's 'KODEX Renewable Energy Active' ETF ranked first in recent 1-month and 3-month returns, demonstrating the growth potential of the renewable energy market [136, 142, 143, 146, 150, 155, 156, 161, 166, 169, 170, 172, 173, 178, 181, 183, 185]. However, LNG combined heat and power generation is being pointed out as a factor hindering the expansion of renewable energy, and the Korea Federation for Environmental Movement is urging the cancellation of new LNG power plant constructions and a direct shift to renewable energy [140, 141, 148, 152, 174]. Meanwhile, the 'renewable energy-tailored' electricity tariff system, effective from the 16th, is a measure to respond to changes in power demand, structured to lower rates during the day when solar power generation is abundant and raise them in the evening [154, 177]. Internationally, South Korea's attempts at renewable energy transition have been highlighted by foreign media in the wake of the Middle East conflict, but instability in the Chinese solar market and the review of US export restrictions could act as variables in the global supply chain [151, 167, 182].

sections.title: ⚙️ AI Technology: A New Driving Force for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology is emerging as a key driving force in responding to climate change. Domestically, AI technology is being introduced to solve pest problems caused by climate change, and a research team at Korea University of Technology and Education has established eco-friendly nanocomposite corrosion inhibition technology using AI [1, 192]. The publication of a book on 'Smart Cities in Response to Climate Change and the AI Era' emphasizes the importance of AI-based urban design [10, 40]. The power grid in the AI era is crucial for national competitiveness and essential for finding carbon neutrality solutions, with the combination of AI and energy emerging as a decisive factor in the carbon neutrality technology race [62, 63, 73, 78]. Discussions on securing AI technological capabilities are also active in political circles, with proposals such as transforming Gyeonggi Province into an AI mega-special zone through AI autonomous driving and regulatory exemptions for renewable energy [157, 159, 189]. Even Allbirds, an eco-friendly shoe company, has declared its transition into an AI company, demonstrating the expanding role of AI across industries [191].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, the climate crisis is approaching at an unprecedented pace, but domestic and international efforts to counter it, along with innovative technological developments, are also actively underway. As climate change spokespersons and science journalists, we pledge to continue closely monitoring these complex phenomena and delivering accurate and timely information. This concludes today's climate briefing.
